volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.3.1123b - Section 4.5.1203)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.3.1123b
sections.original: Curatum id quidemst .
sections.translation: That , indeed , is resolved upon . For if she will not go out of her own accord , I ’ ll turn her out by force .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1125
sections.original: Istuc cave fáxis ; quin potius per gratiam
sections.translation: Do you take care how you do that ; but rather let her go from your house with a good grace [*] , and give her those things that I mentioned . The gold trinkets and apparel , with which you furnished her , let her take away .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1128
sections.original: Cupio hercle .
sections.translation: By my troth , I wish she would .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1128b
sections.original: Credo te facile impetrassere .
sections.translation: I think you ’ ll easily prevail upon her . But go indoors ; don ’ t linger here .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1129b
sections.original: Tibi sum oboediens . —
sections.translation: I obey you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1130
sections.original: Numquid videtur demutare alio atque uti
sections.translation: Now , does he really appear to be anything different from what , awhile ago , I told you he was , this wenching Captain ? Now it is requisite that Acroteleutium should come to me , her maid too , and Pleusicles . O Jupiter ! and does not opportunity favour me in every respect ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1135
sections.original: nam quos videre exoptabam me maxume ,
sections.translation: For those whom I especially wished to see , I perceive at this moment coming out here from our neighbour ’ s .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1137
sections.original: Séquimini , simul círcumspicite , né quis adsit árbiter .
sections.translation: Follow me ; at the same time look around , that there may be no overlooker .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1138
sections.original: Neminem pol video , nísi hunc quem volumus conventum .
sections.translation: Faith , I see no one , only him whom we want to meet .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1138b
sections.original: Et ego vos .
sections.translation: Just as I want you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1139
sections.original: Quid agis , noster architecte ?
sections.translation: How do you do , our master-plotter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1139b
sections.original: Egone architectus ? vah .
sections.translation: I , the master-plotter ? Nonsense .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1139c
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1140
sections.original: Quia enim non sum dignus prae te , ut figam palum in parietem .
sections.translation: Because , in comparison with yourself , I am not worthy to fix a beam in a wall .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1141
sections.original: Heia vero .
sections.translation: Aye , indeed so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1141b
sections.original: Nimis facete nimisque facunde malast .
sections.translation: She ’ s a very fluent and a very clever hand at mischief . How charmingly she did polish off the Captain .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1142b
sections.original: At etiam parum .
sections.translation: But still , not enough .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1143
sections.original: Bono animo es : negotium omne iam succedit sub manus ;
sections.translation: Be of good courage all the business is now prospering under our hands . Only do you , as you have begun , still give a helping hand ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.1145
sections.original: nam ipse miles concubinam intro abiit oratum suam ,
sections.translation: for the Captain himself has gone in-doors , to entreat his mistress to leave his house , with her mother and sister , for Athens .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1146b
sections.original: Eu , probe .
sections.translation: Very good — well done .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1147
sections.original: Quin etiam aurum atque ornamenta , quae ipse instruxit , mulieri
sections.translation: Besides , all the gold trinkets and apparel which he himself has provided for the damsel , he gives her to keep as a present for herself — so have I recommended him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1149
sections.original: Facile istuc quidemst , si et illa volt et ille autem cupit .
sections.translation: Really , it ’ s easily done , if both she wishes it , and he desires it as well .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1150
sections.original: Non tu scis , quom ex alto puteo sursum ad summum escenderis ,
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know that when , from a deep well , you have ascended up to the top , there is the greatest danger lest you should thence fall back again from the top . This affair is now being carried on at the top of the well . If the Captain should have a suspicion of it , nothing whatever of his will be able to be carried off . Now , most especially , we have need of clever contrivances .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1154
sections.original: opust dolis : domi esse ad eam rem video silvai satis ,
sections.translation: I see that there is material enough at home for that purpose —

sections.label: Section 4.4.1155
sections.original: mulieres tres , quartus tute es , quintus ego , sextus senex ;
sections.translation: three women , yourself the fourth , I am the fifth , the old gentleman the sixth .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1156
sections.original: quod apud nos fallaciarum sex situmst , certo scio
sections.translation: What an edifice of stratagems has been erected by us ! I know for certain . that any town seems as though it could be taken by these plans : only do you lend your assistance .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1158b
sections.original: Id nos ad te , si quid velles , venimus .
sections.translation: For that purpose are we come to you , to see if you wish for anything .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1159
sections.original: Lepide facitis . nunc hanc tibi ego ímpero provinciam .
sections.translation: You do what ’ s à propos . Now to you do I assign this department [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1160
sections.original: Impetrabis , imperator , quod ego potero , quod voles .
sections.translation: General , you shall assign me whatever you please , so far as I am capable .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1161
sections.original: Militem lepide , et facete et laute ludificarier
sections.translation: I wish this Captain to be played off cleverly and adroitly .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1162b
sections.original: Voluptatem mecastor mi imperas .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you ’ re assigning me what ’ s a pleasure to me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1162c
sections.original: Scin quem ad modum ?
sections.translation: But do you understand how ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1163
sections.original: Nempe ut adsimulem me amore istius differri .
sections.translation: You mean that I must pretend that I am distracted with love for him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1163b
sections.original: Tenes .
sections.translation: Right — you have it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1164
sections.original: Quasique istius causa amoris ex hoc matrimonio
sections.translation: And as though by reason of that love I had foregone [*] my present marriage , longing for a match with him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1165b
sections.original: Omne ordine .
sections.translation: Everything exactly in its due order ; except only this one point ; you must say that this house ( pointing to the house of PERIPLECOMENUS ) was your marriage-portion ; that the old man had departed hence from you after you had carried out the divorce , lest he should be afraid just now to come here into the house of another man .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1169
sections.original: Bene mones .
sections.translation: You advise me well .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1169b
sections.original: Sed ubi ille exierit intus , istinc te procul
sections.translation: But when he comes out from in-doors , I wish you — standing at a distance there —

sections.label: Section 4.4.1170
sections.original: ita volo adsimulare , prae illius forma quasi spernas tuam
sections.translation: so to make pretence , as though in comparison with his beauty you despised your own , and as though you were struck with awe at his opulent circumstances ; at the same time , too , praise the comeliness of his person , the beauty of his face . Are you tutored enough ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1173b
sections.original: Teneo . satinest , si tibi
sections.translation: I understand it all . Is it enough that I give you my work so nicely finished off that you cannot find a fault with it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1175
sections.original: Sat habeo . nunc tibi vicissim quae imperabo ea discito .
sections.translation: I ’ m content . Now ( addressing PLEUSICLES ) , in your turn , learn what charge I shall give to you . So soon as this shall be done , when she shall have gone in , then do you immediately take care to come here dressed in the garb of a master of a ship . Have on a broad-brimmed hat [*] of iron-grey , a woollen shade [*] before your eyes ; have on an iron-grey cloak [*] ( for that is the seaman ’ s colour );

sections.label: Section 4.4.1180
sections.original: id conexum in umero laevo , exfafillato bracchio ,
sections.translation: have it fastened over the left shoulder , your right arm projecting out [*] , ⋯ your clothes some way well girded up , pretend as though you are some master of a ship . And all these requisites are at the house of this old gentleman , for he keeps fishermen .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1183
sections.original: Quid ? ubi ero exornatus quin tu dicis quid facturus sim ?
sections.translation: Well , when I ’ m dressed out , why don ’ t you tell me what I ’ m to do then ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1184_1185
sections.original: Huc venito et matris verbis Philocomasium arcessito ,
sections.translation: Come here , and , in the name of her mother , bring word to Philocomasium , that , if she would return to Athens , she must go with you to the harbour directly , and that she must order it to be carried down to the ship if she wishes anything to be put on board ; that if she doesn ’ t go , you must weigh anchor , for the wind is favourable .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1189
sections.original: Satis placet pictura . perge .
sections.translation: I like your plan much : do proceed .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1189b
sections.original: Ille extemplo illam hortabitur
sections.translation: The Captain will at once advise her to go speedily that she may not delay her mother .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1190b
sections.original: Multimodis sapis .
sections.translation: Every way you are clever .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1191
sections.original: Ego illi dicam , ut me adiutorem , qui onus feram ad portum , roget .
sections.translation: I shall tell him that she asks for me as a helper to carry her baggage down to the harbour . I shall go , and , understand you , I shall immediately be off with you straight to Athens .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1193b
sections.original: Atque ubi illo veneris ,
sections.translation: And when you have reached there , I ’ ll never let you be ashore three days before you ’ re free .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1195
sections.original: Abi cito atque orna te .
sections.translation: Be off speedily and equip yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1195b
sections.original: Numquid aliud ?
sections.translation: Is there anything besides ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1195c
sections.original: Haec ut memineris .
sections.translation: Only to remember all this .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1196
sections.original: Abeo . —
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1196b
sections.original: Et vos abite hinc intro actutum ; nam illum huc sat scio
sections.translation: And do you ( to ACROTELEUTIUM and MILPHIDIPPA ) be off hence in-doors this instant , for I ’ m quite sure that he ’ ll just now be coming out hence from in-doors .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1197b
sections.original: Celebre apud nos imperium tuomst . —
sections.translation: With us your command is as good as law .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1198
sections.original: Agite abscedite ergo . ecce autem commodum aperitur foris .
sections.translation: Come , then , begone . But see , the door opens opportunely .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1200
sections.original: Quod volui ut volui impetravi , per amicitiam et gratiam ,
sections.translation: What I wished I have obtained just as I wished , on kind and friendly terms , that she would leave me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1201b
sections.original: Quid tam intus fuisse te dicam diu ?
sections.translation: For what reason am I to say that you have been so long in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1202
sections.original: Numquam ego me tam sensi amari quam nunc ab illa muliere .
sections.translation: I never was so sensible that I was beloved by that woman as now .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1203
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: Why so ?
